name: Paho MQTT (Eclipse MQTT Python Client)

auth.notes: Username/password + TLS/SSL certificates for broker auth

performance.notes: MQTT protocol overhead minimal; broker latency dominant

agent_readiness.notes: Standard MQTT Python client. mqttc = mqtt.Client(). mqttc.connect('broker.host', 1883). mqttc.subscribe('topic'). mqttc.on_message callback. mqttc.publish('topic', payload, qos=1). MQTT 3.1.1 and 5.0. TLS/SSL. QoS 0/1/2. Retain messages. Will messages (Last Will). mqttc.loop_forever() blocking or mqttc.loop_start() background thread. Async: aiomqtt for asyncio. Used in IoT, home automation (Home Assistant), telemetry, embedded systems.

gotchas: Callback-based API (not asyncio-native; use aiomqtt for async)
No built-in reconnect with backoff (implement yourself or use reconnect_delay_set)
MQTT broker required (Mosquitto, EMQX, HiveMQ, etc.)
best_when: IoT devices and sensors needing lightweight publish-subscribe
Any MQTT broker integration in Python
avoid_when: Need message persistence/queuing (use RabbitMQ or NATS)
summary: Paho MQTT is the standard Python MQTT client by Eclipse. connect/subscribe/publish/on_message. MQTT 3.1.1 and 5.0. QoS 0/1/2, retain, will messages. Loop-based. Used for IoT, telemetry, Home Assistant. Requires a broker (Mosquitto, EMQX, HiveMQ). Callback-based API — use aiomqtt for asyncio. v2 has breaking changes from v1.
